questions.question: On a one-page summary, where should the headline KPI (e.g. total revenue) go?
questions.explanation: Readers start top-left. Put the single most important number there, big and formatted.

questions.question: Which conditional-formatting option instantly flags repeated customer names or order IDs?
questions.explanation: Duplicate Values highlights repeats — a fast second check after Stage 1's Remove Duplicates.

questions.question: To turn a Region × Quarter grid into a heat map, you use…
questions.explanation: Color Scales shade cells by value, making a grid readable at a glance as a heat map.

questions.question: You want every refunded order's whole row to turn red. Which conditional-formatting rule?
options: New Rule → Use a formula → =$L2="Refunded" → red fill
Data Bars on the Status column
questions.explanation: A formula rule referencing the locked Status column ($L2) applies the format to the entire row.

questions.question: Which custom number format displays 1234567 as "$1.2M"?
questions.explanation: Each comma after the format scales the displayed number by a thousand; the conditions pick M, K, or plain.

questions.question: In the capstone, why prefer a live formula (e.g. SUMIFS) over typing the number you read off a pivot?
options: The answer stays correct if the data changes or is refreshed
questions.explanation: A live formula recalculates when the data updates; a hard-typed number silently goes stale.
